conventions_testing_762773408812.content: Tests live co-located next to route files as route.test.ts, not in a separate __tests__ dir.

conventions_api_routes_762774714190.content: Response envelope is always { success, data } or { success: false, error: string } — never raw throw/catch.

facts_api_routes_762775940179.content: When implementing a submit endpoint, validate existence first (404), then business rules (400), then duplicates (409), before creating (201). Order matters for correct error precedence.

thoughts_auth_943550408574.content: Accepting studentId from request body allows any authenticated student to impersonate another student — always use user.id from the authenticated session instead

thoughts_srcappapiassignmentsidsubmit_94.content: A check-then-insert pattern (find duplicate, then create) has a race condition window where concurrent requests can both pass the check and insert duplicates

conventions_auth_943553543274.content: Use withAuth middleware to gate endpoints and extract user.id from the authenticated context — never trust client-supplied identity fields

facts_data_integrity_943555143235.content: Duplicate submission protection must use a database constraint or transaction, not a find-then-insert in two separate calls

facts_srcappapi_943556575592.content: The reference pattern for quiz submission (findByID validation + {success, data} response shape) is correct and should be replicated, but auth must use user.id not body params

facts_auth_1330842406635.content: The authenticated user identity must come from the session or token, never from client-supplied body parameters — body studentId should be ignored or validated against the auth identity.

facts_srcappapiassignmentsidsubmitroutet.content: To prevent race-condition duplicate submissions, use a unique database constraint on (assignmentId, studentId) and handle the constraint violation error, rather than a check-then-insert pattern.

thoughts_submissions_schema_141331859711.content: If a route returns 409 for duplicate submissions, the DB unique constraint must actually exist in the schema — code comments asserting it are not enough.

facts_api_route_patterns_1413320068605.content: Quiz submit pattern (getPayload, withAuth, findByID, {success,data}/{success:false,error}) is the canonical reference for entity-submission endpoints in this codebase.

thoughts_authsubmission_routes_141332142.content: For submission endpoints, always use user.id from the authenticated context (withAuth) rather than studentId from the request body to prevent auth bypass.

thoughts_srcappapiassignmentsidsubmitrou.content: 409 duplicate-detection logic that relies on a DB unique constraint is fragile — if the constraint doesn't exist in the schema, concurrent submissions silently create duplicates. Always verify the con

facts_auth_1567673003186.content: studentId must come from the authenticated user context (user.id), never from the request body, to prevent auth bypass.

conventions_srcappapi_1567674803241.content: This codebase uses getPayload({ config: configPromise }) and withAuth middleware for API routes, not direct payload access.

conventions_api_responses_1567676239726.content: Success responses follow { success, data } shape; errors follow { success: false, error } — matching the quiz submit pattern.

thoughts_srcappapiassignmentsidsubmitrou.content: Tests using PostgreSQL constraint names (e.g., 'submissions_assignment_student_unique') will break on different backends; abstract the error or mock generically.

facts_submissions_collection_16922381053.content: Duplicate submission detection is delegated to the DB unique constraint on (assignment, student) — no pre-check needed, race-safe by default.

conventions_api_routes_1692239604027.content: Student-facing endpoints read user identity from withAuth context (user.id), never from the request body — body carries only business data.
